methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $67M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Sift Stack is a Series B AI & Software company that has raised $67M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR) operating in the AI & Software sector at similar or adjacent funding stages. Key private peers include Figure AI ($39.0B, Series C), Reflection AI ($25.0B, Series A), Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series A), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), and Together AI ($8.3B, Series A).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $67M total capital raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $127M. The peer-median method benchmarks against seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $14.0B (range: $3.2B–$39.0B), implying $14.0B. The wide dispersion reflects significant heterogeneity in frontier-tech valuations at this stage.

The triangulated analysis yields a low of $108M, median of $7.1B, and high of $16.1B. The two-method split reflects material uncertainty about whether Sift Stack's trajectory aligns with early-stage standouts or closer to the stage-typical median. Key uncertainties include company-specific unit economics, revenue scale relative to peers, and category maturation risk within frontier AI.
